questions.question: What do plants need to grow?
questions.explanation: Plants need sunlight and water to grow. That is why we water plants and put them near windows!

questions.question: What is the colour of the sky on a sunny day?
questions.explanation: The sky looks blue on a sunny day because of the way sunlight travels through the air.

questions.question: Which animal lays eggs?
questions.explanation: Birds lay eggs! Baby birds hatch out of eggs.

questions.question: What do we call frozen water?
questions.explanation: When water gets very cold it freezes and becomes ice!

questions.question: How many legs does a spider have?
questions.explanation: Spiders have 8 legs! That is two more than insects, which have 6.

questions.question: What sound does a dog make?
questions.explanation: Dogs bark or woof! Each animal has its own special sound.

questions.question: Which of these can fly?
questions.explanation: Birds have wings and can fly. Fish swim, and dogs and cats walk on land.

questions.question: Which season is the hottest?
questions.explanation: Summer is the hottest season. The sun shines longer and the days are warm!

questions.question: What do we use our eyes for?
questions.explanation: We use our eyes to see! Our five senses are sight, hearing, smell, touch and taste.

questions.question: Which of these is a living thing?
questions.explanation: A flower is a living thing — it grows, needs water and sunlight, and is part of a plant.

questions.question: What do caterpillars turn into?
questions.explanation: Caterpillars go into a cocoon and change into butterflies! This is called metamorphosis.

questions.question: Which season has the most snow?
questions.explanation: Winter is the coldest season and is most likely to bring snow and frost!

questions.question: What animal lives in the sea and has fins?
questions.explanation: Fish live in the sea and rivers, and use their fins to swim through the water.

questions.question: What do we breathe in to stay alive?
questions.explanation: We breathe in air, and our lungs take out the oxygen we need to live.

questions.question: Which of these is a fruit that grows on a tree?
questions.explanation: Apples grow on apple trees! Carrots and potatoes grow underground, and onions are bulbs.

questions.question: What do cows give us to drink?
questions.explanation: Cows produce milk, which farmers collect for us to drink and use to make cheese and butter!

questions.question: What comes out of the sky when it rains?
questions.explanation: Rain is water that falls from clouds in the sky. It helps plants grow and fills our rivers!

questions.question: Which of these animals has a shell?
questions.explanation: A snail carries a hard shell on its back to protect its soft body!

questions.question: What does the Sun give us?
questions.explanation: The Sun gives us light and warmth. Without the Sun, it would be dark and very cold!

questions.question: What noise does a lion make?
questions.explanation: Lions roar! It is one of the loudest sounds in the animal kingdom.
